title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

title: What Happened When Meta Used A.I. to Ban Accounts on Facebook and Instagram
summary: Users said the technology mistakenly deleted their accounts. To resolve the issue, they still had to rely on A.I.

title: Google Releases Three New Gemini A.I. Models
summary: The models include one that is the company’s most powerful and another that is fine-tuned for cybersecurity, as Google competes with rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
summary: As Google incorporates more artificial intelligence into search, people are spending more time on Google. Some website operators are crying foul.

title: When Listening to Music Was a Team Sport
summary: An exhibition at the Cooper Hewitt celebrates the glorious machines that gave us the soundtracks of our lives before streaming sites shoved them toward obsolescence.

title: Judge Temporarily Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
summary: The delay is intended to give the court time to consider a lawsuit that argues the $111 billion merger violates antitrust laws.

title: A.I. ‘Vibecoded’ Apps Are Flooding Apple’s App Store
summary: Mobile apps are easy to make with artificial intelligence. That doesn’t mean people are excited to use them.

title: Why Silicon Valley Can’t Stop Looking Over Its Shoulder at China
summary: Chinese companies are offering artificial intelligence that is nearly as good as the leading U.S. technologies, but it’s still America’s race to lose.

title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

title: The ‘Bad Blood’ Between Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi’s Tarek Mansour
summary: Shayne Coplan of Polymarket and Tarek Mansour of Kalshi are young billionaires intent on dominating prediction markets. It’s more than a battle. It’s “bad blood.”

title: Your ‘Live’ World Cup Broadcast Is Not Really Live
summary: Everyone is watching the goals after they happen, and some get them significantly faster.

title: Meta in Talks to Lease Computing Power to Anthropic in Potential $10 Billion Deal
summary: A deal would underline how scarce computing power is for artificial intelligence development, and could create a new business for Meta.

title: Roger Summit, Who Invented an Early Online Search Service, Dies at 95
summary: A research scientist at Lockheed, he came up with the idea for a computer system that would search scientific and technical literature almost instantaneously.

title: The A.I. Trade Secrets War + Economists Say ‘We Must Act Now’ + HatGPT
summary: “I don’t know if the public understands this, but these companies hate each other.”

title: White House Teleprompter Operator Bet on Trump Speeches, Kalshi Says
summary: A technical assistant to President Trump won around $100,000, according to the prediction market, which flagged the activity to the federal government.

title: Mary-Dell Chilton Dies at 87; Helped Create First Genetically Modified Plant
summary: In 1982, she led the research team that figured out how to genetically alter plants, a discovery that would eventually transform global agriculture.

title: Someone Used A.I. to Write an Unauthorized Biography of Me. I Don’t Recommend Reading It.
summary: Someone used A.I. to write my biography. Thousands more of those books are polluting Amazon. Who is behind all this drivel?

title: Google Ordered to Give A.I. Rivals More Access on Android Smartphones
summary: The decision by European Union regulators is a response to fears that Google will use its vast Android user base to gain an edge in A.I.

title: The Quest for ‘Technological Sovereignty’ in Europe (and Why It’s So Hard)
summary: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.

title: Judge Dismisses Suit Accusing Apple of Failing to Limit Child Sex Abuse Material
summary: The lawsuit highlighted concerns that the privacy of Apple’s iCloud allows illegal material to be easily stored and shared on the service.
